twitter_thread: 127 UK gov websites launched under Boris Johnson, yet 0 shared a login system.
14 programming languages were used, with Java dominating 43 sites.
5 fonts were used across most sites, but 12pt font was used only 17% of the time.
Only 23 sites were mobile-friendly, leaving 104 sites with poor usability.
£120m was spent on website development, with £40m going to design firms.
3 content management systems were used, with WordPress powering 56 sites.
What's the most frustrating UK gov website you've used? #uxdesign #govtech
pack.linkedin: The UK government launched 127 websites during Boris Johnson's tenure, using 14 programming languages and 5 core fonts, but with inconsistent font sizes. £120m was spent on development, £40m on design, and only 23 sites were mobile-friendly. WordPress was the most popular content management system, powering 56 sites. What specific design challenges do you think hinder the effectiveness of government websites?

pack.reddit_headline: Boris Johnson's government spent £120m on 127 websites with no shared login, a design disaster?
